title: Scalar throughput: example.upper_case(text)
description: String scalar throughput with varying payload sizes per row.
setup_sql: CREATE OR REPLACE TEMP TABLE bench_input AS SELECT repeat('a', ${param:payload_bytes}) AS s FROM range(${param:rows}) t(i);

payload.notes: Arrow string layout: 4-byte offset + payload_bytes content; output length == input length for ASCII uppercase.

notes: Uses count(upper_case(s)) — COUNT(expr) requires the expression to be evaluated per row (it counts non-NULLs), forcing the worker to actually be called. Use bytes/sec — rows/sec is misleading across payload sizes.
